enriched_points.expanded_content: Natural disasters lead to immediate injuries and long-term health issues, including chronic diseases, mental health disorders, and increased mortality rates due to disrupted healthcare services.
Disaster management encompasses preparedness, response, recovery, and mitigation phases, ensuring effective resource allocation and minimizing health impacts on affected populations.
Effective strategies include improving public health infrastructure, enhancing community resilience, and conducting risk assessments to reduce vulnerability to future disasters.
Timely and coordinated efforts during the response phase can significantly reduce morbidity and mortality, while recovery involves restoring healthcare access and addressing long-term health needs.

Intersectoral collaboration among healthcare, emergency services, and community organizations is crucial for comprehensive disaster preparedness and response, ensuring equitable access to medical care.
